questions.text: The name of first book of Pushto language is:
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: "Pata Khazana" is the first book in the Pashto language. It covers various aspects of Pashto language, literature, history, poetry, folk songs, stories, humor, social issues, literary history, prominent figures, literary movements, and historical events. It provides a wealth of information about the Pashto language and its diverse facets.

questions.text: First Five Year Plan of Pakistan was launched in:
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The first Five Year Plan of Pakistan was launched in 1955. It was a comprehensive economic development plan that aimed to promote industrialization, improve infrastructure, and enhance the overall economic growth of the country. The plan focused on sectors such as agriculture, manufacturing, energy, and transportation. It laid the foundation for future development strategies in Pakistan.

questions.text: In which year Punjab government Protection Act against violence was passed in: ں
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The Punjab government passed the Protection of Women Against Violence Act in 2016. This act aimed to provide legal protection to women against various forms of violence, including domestic violence, emotional abuse, stalking, and cyber harassment. It also established protection centers and helplines to support and assist victims of violence. The act was a significant step towards safeguarding the rights and well-being of women in Punjab.

questions.text: The length of common border between Pakistan and Afganistan is:
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The length of the common border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is approximately 2,430 kilometers. This border spans across different regions of Pakistan, including Balochistan, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, and FATA, as well as various provinces in Afghanistan, such as Kandahar, Helmand, Nimruz, Farah, Herat, Badghis, and Faryab. The border serves as a boundary between Pakistan and Afghanistan, connecting the two countries.

questions.text: Who led the Khilafat Movement?
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The Khilafat Movement was led by Maulana Muhammad Ali Johar, Maulana Shaukat Ali Johar, and Maulana Abul Kalam Azad. The purpose of the Khilafat Movement was to support the caliphate of Muslims and to advocate for the protection of the Khilafat system in Turkey.

questions.text: Pakistan became member of United Nations in:
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: Pakistan became a member of the United Nations in 1949, shortly after gaining independence from British rule. As a new nation, Pakistan sought to establish its presence on the international stage and contribute to global peace and development. Joining the United Nations allowed Pakistan to engage in diplomatic relations with other countries and participate in international forums to address various global issues.

questions.text: The total area of Pakistan is <Qaeure kilometers.
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The total area of Pakistan is approximately 796,095 square kilometers. Pakistan has a diverse landscape, with notable features such as the Himalayan mountains, the Thar Desert, the fertile plains of the Indus River, and coastal areas. The vast land of Pakistan is utilized for various purposes including agriculture, mining, and tourism.

questions.text: Objective Resolution was passed in:
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: Objective Resolution was passed in Pakistan on March 12, 1949. It was a significant constitutional milestone that laid the foundation for the future Constitution of Pakistan. The resolution aimed to establish an Islamic framework for the country while ensuring the protection of the rights of religious minorities. It emphasized principles such as democracy, social justice, and equality. The Objective Resolution played a crucial role in shaping the subsequent constitutional developments in Pakistan.

questions.text: Tenure of National Assembly is:
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The tenure of the National Assembly in Pakistan is five years. After every general election, members of the National Assembly are elected to serve for a term of five years, unless dissolved earlier. During this period, the National Assembly plays a vital role in legislation, governance, and representing the interests of the people.
